title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy scalp
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy scalp are natural remedies made from infusions of plants and herbs. They help alleviate itchiness by soothing the scalp, reducing inflammation, and fighting against fungal or bacterial infections. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for an itchy scalp include chamomile, nettle, and calendula. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce itching and irritation, while nettle is rich in nutrients that promote hair growth and soothe the scalp. Calendula, on the other hand, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help treat scalp infections and dandruff. By using herbal teas for an itchy scalp, individuals can experience relief from discomfort, improved scalp health, and enhanced hair growth, leading to an overall improvement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal an irritated scalp. The tea's anti-inflammatory agents help reduce redness, irritation, and swelling, while its antimicrobial properties can help eliminate any fungal or bacterial causes of itchiness. Additionally, chamomile is a natural anti-inflammatory and can help alleviate dryness and itching by stimulating the scalp's circulation, promoting hair growth, and leaving the hair soft and shiny. Its soothing and calming effects can provide instant relief to an itchy scalp, making it an excellent natural remedy for this common issue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea can reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy scalp as it helps alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and irritation. This property is attributed to the active constituent, chamazulene.

Antimicrobial: It can help inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which is crucial for itchy scalp as it may prevent infections that can worsen the condition. The antimicrobial properties come from the compound matricin, which is converted to chamazulene.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing itchiness. The antioxidant effects are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids.

Soothing: The tea has a calming effect on the skin, which can help alleviate itchiness and discomfort. This soothing property is attributed to the flavonoid apigenin, which has anti-pruritic (anti-itch) effects.

Wound healing: German chamomile tea can help speed up wound healing, which is important for itchy scalp as it may reduce the risk of developing scabs or sores from excessive scratching. The wound-healing properties are attributed to the flavonoids and essential oils found in the plant.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or loose leaf tea bag.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, allowing them to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag from the water.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Slowly pour the tea over your scalp, massaging it in gently, and letting it sit for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils, chamomile flowers are used to make medicinal teas for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Containing various therapeutic compounds, chamomile leaves are used in teas to soothe itchy scalp, as they have mild astringent and anti-inflammatory effects.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are a source of beneficial oils and are used in teas to help alleviate itchy scalp, as they possess soothing and moisturizing properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with itchy scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe inflammation and irritation, while also helping to combat any potential fungal or bacterial infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. Additionally, pot marigold is rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have been shown to support scalp health by promoting blood circulation and encouraging hair growth. The tea can be used as a rinse after shampooing, and its calendula extract can help to balance the scalp's natural oils, reducing excess oiliness or dryness that can lead to itching. Overall, pot marigold tea can provide a natural and soothing solution for an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea can help reduce inflammation on the scalp, providing relief from itchiness caused by skin irritations, due to its high content of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help maintain a clean and healthy scalp by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause itchiness and dandruff, thanks to the presence of active compounds like flavonoids and essential oils.

Antipruritic: Pot marigold tea has a soothing effect on itchy skin, reducing the urge to scratch, which can further damage the scalp and worsen itching, due to the anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of its active constituents.

Emollient: The emollient properties of pot marigold tea help moisturize and soften the scalp, alleviating dryness and flakiness, which can contribute to itchiness. This is achieved through the presence of mucilaginous compounds and essential oils in the plant.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help repair damaged skin on the scalp and prevent further itching and irritation. This is attributed to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids, triterpenes, and carotenoids, which have been shown to support skin healing and repair.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried pot marigold flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers and let it cool down.
Massage the tea onto your scalp, leave it on for 15-20 minutes, then rinse it out.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them ideal for soothing an itchy scalp.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have been used in teas to treat skin irritations, including itchy scalp, due to their calming and soothing effects.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been used in teas to promote scalp health and alleviate itchiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and reduce inflammation. The tea is made from the flowers of the Lavandula angustifolia plant, which contains compounds that can help to eliminate skin-irritating bacteria and fungi. Additionally, its calming scent can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can further help to alleviate itchiness and discomfort. By massaging lavender tea into the scalp, the nutrients and antioxidants in the tea can be absorbed into the skin, providing additional benefits for hair and scalp health. Overall, English lavender tea is a natural and effective remedy for treating an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, providing relief from itchiness. This property is due to the presence of compounds like linalyl acetate and linalool.

Antimicrobial: It has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, preventing infections that can cause itchiness. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are eucalyptol, camphor, and limonene.
Anti-pruritic: English lavender tea has an anti-pruritic effect, which helps alleviate itching sensations. This property is attributed to the presence of linalool and lavandulol.

Analgesic: It has analgesic properties that help relieve pain and discomfort associated with an itchy scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property include cineole, linalyl acetate, and geraniol.

Soothing: English lavender tea has a soothing effect on the skin, which can help calm an itchy scalp. This property is due to the combined action of various active constituents, including linalool, linalyl acetate, and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, bring 2 cups of water to a boil, then add 2 tablespoons of dried English lavender flowers.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea into a bowl, discarding the spent lavender flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature, or refrigerate it to speed up the process.
Massage the cooled lavender tea into your scalp, leaving it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing with water.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in soothing compounds, the flowers of English lavender are used to make medicinal teas for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Containing essential oils, lavender leaves are used in teas to help alleviate itchy scalp by providing calming and antipruritic effects.
Buds: High in antioxidants, lavender buds are included in medicinal teas for itchy scalp to support skin cell regeneration and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and anti-microbial properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and promote hair growth. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, irritation, and itching, while its anti-microbial properties can help to combat any fungal or bacterial issues that may be contributing to the itchiness. Additionally, rosemary tea can help to improve circulation in the scalp, which can promote hair growth and a healthier scalp. The tea's soothing and calming effects can provide relief from itchiness and discomfort, leaving the scalp feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, providing relief from itchiness. This property is important for itchy scalp as inflammation can worsen the itchiness and cause discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include carnosic acid and carnosol.

Antimicrobial: Rosemary tea has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp, preventing infections that can cause itchiness. This property is important for itchy scalp as microbial infections can lead to scalp irritation and itchiness. The active constituents responsible for this property include eucalyptol, camphor, and alpha-pinene.

Anti-pruritic: Rosemary tea has anti-pruritic properties that help alleviate itching and reduce the urge to scratch. This property is important for itchy scalp as scratching can damage the scalp and lead to further irritation and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.

Circulation-boosting: Rosemary tea helps improve blood circulation to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing inflammation. This property is important for itchy scalp as improved circulation can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. This property is important for itchy scalp as oxidative stress can worsen inflammation and cause itchiness. The active constituents responsible for this property include carnosic acid, carnosol, and rosmarinic acid.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves, letting it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the rosemary leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the rosemary tea over your scalp, massaging it gently, and let it sit for 15-20 minutes before rinsing it off.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of rosemary are commonly used in making medicinal teas for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain high levels of antioxidants and have been shown to soothe irritated skin, making them a popular choice for treating itchy scalp.
Stems: The stems of rosemary contain essential oils that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing relief for itchy scalp.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help to nourish and soothe the scalp, reducing itchiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with itchy scalp because it has antifungal and antiseptic properties that can help to combat scalp irritation and inflammation. Thyme contains compounds such as thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to have potent antimicrobial effects against various strains of bacteria and fungi. These properties make thyme tea an effective natural remedy for treating itchy scalp caused by dandruff, seborrheic dermatitis, or other scalp conditions. Additionally, thyme tea can help to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing inflammation. By using thyme tea as a rinse or applying it directly to the scalp, you can soothe and relieve itchiness, leaving your scalp feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme tea has the ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, including those that can cause scalp irritation and itchiness, due to its active constituents such as thymol and carvacrol.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, which can alleviate itching and discomfort, due to its active constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchiness and other scalp issues, due to its active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and luteolin.

Antifungal properties: Thyme tea has been shown to have antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections on the scalp, which can cause itchiness and other symptoms, due to its active constituents such as thymol and terpinen-4-ol.

Circulation-boosting properties: Thyme tea can help improve blood flow to the scalp, which can promote healthy hair growth and reduce itchiness, due to its active constituents such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 5-10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried thyme leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the thyme leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a rinse on your scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antifungal and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing an itchy scalp.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate itchiness and irritation on the scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with itchy scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe inflammation and reduce irritation on the scalp. Its antioxidant properties can also help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall scalp health. Additionally, lemon balm tea has been shown to help balance the pH level of the scalp, which can help to reduce dandruff and other scalp irritations. Drinking lemon balm tea can also provide internal benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to a healthy scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief for itchy scalp. This property is important because itchy scalp is often caused by inflammation, and reducing inflammation can help alleviate the itch. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tea has antimicrobial properties that help fight bacteria and fungi, preventing infections that can cause itchy scalp. This property is important because infections can exacerbate itchy scalp, and eliminating the source of infection can help alleviate the itch. The active constituents responsible for this property include citral and citronellal.

Antioxidant: Lemon balm tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp from oxidative stress, reducing damage and inflammation. This property is important because oxidative stress can contribute to itchy scalp, and reducing oxidative stress can help alleviate the itch. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Analgesic: Lemon balm tea has analgesic properties that help relieve pain and discomfort, providing relief for itchy scalp. This property is important because itchy scalp can be painful and uncomfortable, and reducing pain and discomfort can help alleviate the itch. The active constituents responsible for this property include eugenol and citral.

Sedative: Lemon balm tea has sedative properties that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, reducing stress and tension that can exacerbate itchy scalp. This property is important because stress and tension can contribute to itchy scalp, and reducing stress and tension can help alleviate the itch. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and citronellal.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried lemon balm leaves, letting it steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the lemon balm leaves, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Massage the tea into the scalp, ensuring even coverage.
Leave the tea on the scalp for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out with warm water.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in teas for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain high levels of antioxidants, making them useful for soothing an itchy scalp and promoting healthy hair growth.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, making them helpful for reducing itchiness and irritation on the scalp.
Seeds: Lemon balm seeds are rich in essential oils, which can help to moisturize and soothe a dry, itchy scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can soothe an irritated scalp and help to reduce inflammation. Sage is also known to regulate the production of oil in the scalp, which can help to prevent excess oil and buildup that can lead to itchiness. Additionally, sage contains antioxidants that can help to protect the scalp from damage and promote healthy hair growth. Drinking sage tea or using it as a rinse can provide relief from an itchy scalp and promote overall scalp health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea can help reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy scalp as inflammation can worsen the itchiness and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Sage tea has antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp, which can cause itchiness. The active constituents that contribute to this property include camphor, tannins, and volatile oils.

Antipruritic: Sage tea has a soothing effect on the skin, which can help alleviate itching. This property is important for itchy scalp as it directly addresses the main symptom. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Astringent: Sage tea can help tighten and tone the skin, which can help reduce itchiness and flakiness on the scalp. This property is important for itchy scalp as it can help remove excess oil and impurities from the scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and various volatile oils.

Circulation-boosting: Sage tea can help improve blood circulation, which can promote healthy skin and hair growth. This property is important for itchy scalp as improved circulation can help deliver essential nutrients and oxygen to the scalp, promoting overall scalp health. The active constituents responsible for this property include α- and β-thujone, as well as various flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried sage leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and let the sage tea steep for an additional 15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the sage leaves, then let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour the sage tea over your scalp, massage it gently, and let it sit for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy scalp.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, they help promote scalp health and alleviate itching.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, they can moisturize the scalp, reducing itchiness and flaking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and combat dandruff-causing fungus. The tea is rich in thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation. Additionally, oregano tea can help to balance the pH levels of the scalp, creating an unfavorable environment for bacteria and fungi that can cause itchiness and flaking. Regular use of oregano tea as a rinse or treatment can provide relief from an itchy scalp and promote a healthy scalp and hair.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Oregano tea helps with itchy scalp by inhibiting the growth of fungi that can cause scalp irritation, due to its active constituents thymol and carvacrol.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Oregano tea alleviates itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and redness, thanks to its rosmarinic acid content.
Antioxidant properties: Oregano tea promotes scalp health by neutralizing free radicals and protecting skin cells from damage, owing to its high levels of antioxidants like carvacrol and thymol.

Antibacterial properties: Oregano tea helps with itchy scalp by eliminating bacteria that can cause infections and further irritation, attributable to its active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol.
Analgesic properties: Oregano tea soothes itchy scalp by providing pain relief, which can be linked to its active constituents, such as thymol and carvacrol.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried oregano leaves, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
After simmering, strain the oregano tea into a bowl, discarding the oregano leaves.
Allow the oregano tea to cool down to a warm temperature that is comfortable for your scalp.
Slowly pour the oregano tea over your scalp, massaging it gently into your scalp for 5-10 minutes.
Rinse out the oregano tea with warm water, making sure to remove all residue from your scalp and hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of oregano are commonly used in medicinal teas for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: The flowers of oregano contain high levels of antioxidants, making them useful for soothing an itchy scalp.
Seeds: Oregano seeds are rich in essential oils, which can help to alleviate itching and reduce inflammation on the scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with itchy scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and combat any potential infections. It is rich in silica, a mineral that can strengthen hair and improve its overall health, leading to a reduction in scalp irritation. The tea also contains antioxidants that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, providing relief from itchiness. Additionally, horsetail tea can help to balance the pH level of the scalp, creating an unfavorable environment for bacteria and fungi that can cause itchiness. These benefits make horsetail tea a natural and effective remedy for an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb; let it simmer for 15 minutes.
After simmering, remove the pot from heat and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the horsetail herb, pouring it into a clean container.
Shampoo your scalp and rinse thoroughly.
Use the horsetail tea as a final rinse, massaging it into your scalp, leaving it on for 5-10 minutes before rinsing it out.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea reduces inflammation, which helps alleviate itching and irritation on the scalp. This property is due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea inhibits the growth of microorganisms, helping to prevent scalp infections that can cause itchiness. The antimicrobial activity comes from the tea's content of phenolic acids and other antimicrobial agents.
Astringent: Horsetail tea tightens and constricts tissues, reducing scalp irritation and itchiness. The astringent properties are attributed to the presence of tannins in horsetail.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea neutralizes free radicals, promoting scalp health and reducing itchiness. The antioxidant effects are due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other antioxidant compounds.

Anti-pruritic: Horsetail tea directly reduces itchiness by inhibiting the release of histamine and other pruritogenic factors. This anti-pruritic property is due to the presence of silica and other active constituents in horsetail.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica and other minerals, making them effective in soothing itchy scalp and promoting hair growth.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, helping to alleviate itchiness and reduce scalp irritation.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are a great source of antioxidants and have been used in teas to help soothe itchy scalp and support overall scalp health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe inflammation and reduce itching. It is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin A, C, and E, which are essential for healthy hair and scalp. Dandelion tea also has antibacterial properties that can help to eliminate any bacteria or fungus that may be causing the itchiness. Furthermore, it can help to balance the pH level of the scalp, creating an unfavorable environment for danderm or other microorganisms that can cause itchiness. Drinking dandelion tea or using it as a rinse can provide relief from an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tea can help reduce itchiness and inflammation on the scalp, thanks to its high concentration of flavonoids and polyphenols.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of dandelion tea, attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones, can help control scalp irritation and infections contributing to itchiness.
Antioxidant: Dandelion tea contains potent antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, that can help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and reduce itchiness.
Diuretic: The diuretic properties of dandelion tea, due to its taraxacin and taraxacerin content, can help eliminate toxins and reduce inflammation in the scalp, thus alleviating itchiness.
Nutrient-rich: Dandelion tea is packed with essential vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin A, B, C, and iron, that promote scalp health and help soothe itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of fresh dandelion flowers or 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion flowers.
Rinse the fresh flowers under cold water to remove any dirt or insects.
Steep the dandelion flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, reserving the liquid.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature before gently massaging it into the scalp. Allow it to sit for 5-10 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions are used in making medicinal teas for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe scalp irritation.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are rich in vitamins and minerals that can help nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing itchiness.
Flowers: Dandelion flowers contain antioxidants that can help protect the scalp from damage and reduce inflammation, providing relief from itchiness.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds are a good source of essential fatty acids that can help moisturize the scalp, reducing dryness and itchiness.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy scalp with herbal teas is by using chamomile or peppermint tea. These teas have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate itching and irritation. Brew a strong tea, let it cool, and then use it as a rinse after shampooing. Gently massage the tea into your scalp, allowing it to sit for several minutes before rinsing. Repeat this process regularly for best results. Additionally, a tea rinse can help maintain a healthy scalp and promote hair growth.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal teas include dandruff and seborrheic dermatitis. Chamomile tea, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, can help soothe an itchy and irritated scalp. Meanwhile, green tea, rich in antioxidants, can promote hair growth and reduce inflammation. Peppermint tea can help relieve symptoms of dandruff and soothe the scalp due to its cooling and anti-inflammatory effects. It is important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief for certain scalp conditions, they should not replace medical treatment, especially for severe or persistent symptoms. Always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
